title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers ports, immutable values, promise ownership, and lifecycle into a changed explicitly owned crawler component graph. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of explicitly owned crawler component graph with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Create readonly branded identities only after unknown runtime values pass current validation and normalization.
Assign each Promise a caller, settlement path, concurrency slot, cancellation rule, error destination, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Directed Ports
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 2206 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
